basic_info.about: Established in 1853, the University of Melbourne is a public research university located in Melbourne, Australia. It is Australia's second oldest university and the oldest in Victoria.
basic_info.summary: Consistently ranked as the top university in Australia, it is a member of the Group of Eight and is renowned for its research output and the 'Melbourne Curriculum' which adopts an interdisciplinary approach to higher education.

accreditations.body: Tertiary Education Quality and Standards Agency (TEQSA)

basic_info.affiliations: Group of Eight, Universitas 21, Association of Pacific Rim Universities (APRU), Association of Commonwealth Universities (ACU)
basic_info.recognition: Recognized globally for academic excellence; holds 9 Nobel laureates among staff and alumni.

placements.graduate_outcomes_note: Placement rate reflects the overall undergraduate employment rate. Median salary is for domestic undergraduates; postgraduates earn a median of 100,000 AUD.

placements.placement_highlights: The University of Melbourne continues to lead in graduate employability, ranking 8th globally in QS rankings. While overall undergraduate employment saw a slight dip in 2024 due to easing labor market conditions, median salaries for full-time graduates increased by over 5%. High-demand sectors like Law, Engineering, and Finance report the strongest salary outcomes and recruiter presence.

fees.fees_note: Tuition fees for international students are calculated based on the specific course and study load, with 2026 indicative annual rates typically ranging from AUD 38,000 to over AUD 120,000 for specialized clinical programs. Living expenses, including university-managed accommodation and essentials, are estimated to be at least AUD 29,710 per year as per Australian visa requirements.

scholarships_detail.name: Graduate Research Scholarship
scholarships_detail.eligibility: High-achieving domestic and international students enrolling in graduate research degrees (Masters by Research or PhD).

scholarships_detail.name: Melbourne International Undergraduate Scholarship
scholarships_detail.eligibility: International students from eligible low/middle-income countries with high secondary school results; provides partial fee remission.

scholarships_detail.name: Melbourne Chancellor's Scholarship
scholarships_detail.eligibility: High-achieving international students who have completed secondary school in Australia or an Australian foundation program.

infrastructure.details: Main campus featuring historic sandstone buildings, 12 libraries, gyms, and the new Student Precinct.
Purpose-built for creative arts with sound/dance stages, animation studios, and cinemas in Melbourne's arts precinct.
Innovation hub for digital talent, emerging researchers, and industry colocation.
Opening in 2026 as a center for large-scale experimental research and industry collaboration.
Includes 7-Tesla MRI machines, Maker Spaces (NExT Lab, FabLab), and the Extreme Air-Sea Interaction Facility.

hostel_details.type: Residential Colleges and University Accommodation (Little Hall, The Lofts, Lisa Bellear House)

scholarships.name: Melbourne Research Scholarship (Stipend)
scholarships.eligibility: High-achieving domestic and international students enrolled in a Masters by Research or Doctoral degree.

scholarships.name: Melbourne International Undergraduate Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: International students with excellent secondary school results from a country with GDP per capita ≤ $10,000.

scholarships.name: Hansen Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Year 12 students in Australia demonstrating financial need and high academic achievement.

scholarships.name: Melbourne Graduate Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Students enrolled in eligible graduate coursework programs; value varies (typically 25% or 50% fee remission).

scholarships.name: Australia Awards Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: International students from participating partner countries for full-time study.
